children.name: Chionis
children.description: The only Antarctic bird family without webbed feet, sheathbills are stout, pigeon-like shorebirds named for the horny sheath covering the base of their bill. They are opportunistic scavengers that follow penguin colonies and seal haul-outs, feeding on eggs, carrion, and any organic debris. Their white plumage provides camouflage against the snow and ice of their harsh sub-Antarctic and Antarctic home.
